1→"""Tests for FakeCommandExecutor.""" 2→ 3→from tests.fakes.gateway.command_executor import FakeCommandExecutor 4→ 5→ 6→def test_tracks_opened_urls() -> None: 7→ """Fake executor tracks URLs that were opened.""" 8→ executor = FakeCommandExecutor() 9→ executor.open_url("https://example.com") 10→ executor.open_url("https://github.com") 11→ assert executor.opened_urls == ["https://example.com", "https://github.com"] 12→ 13→ 14→def test_tracks_copied_texts() -> None: 15→ """Fake executor tracks texts that were copied to clipboard.""" 16→ executor = FakeCommandExecutor() 17→ executor.copy_to_clipboard("erk implement 123") 18→ executor.copy_to_clipboard("erk pr dispatch 456") 19→ assert executor.copied_texts == ["erk implement 123", "erk pr dispatch 456"] 20→ 21→ 22→def test_tracks_closed_plans() -> None: 23→ """Fake executor tracks plans that were closed.""" 24→ executor = FakeCommandExecutor() 25→ executor.close_plan(123, "https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123") 26→ executor.close_plan(456, "https://github.com/test/repo/issues/456") 27→ assert executor.closed_plans == [ 28→ (123, "https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123"), 29→ (456, "https://github.com/test/repo/issues/456"), 30→ ]

## Summary
I've successfully updated the test assertion strings in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/fakes/test_fake_command_executor.py`:
**Changes made:** - **Line 37**: Changed `executor.notify("Closed plan #456", severity=None)` notification call - **Line 40**: Changed assertion from `"Closed plan #456"` to `"Closed PR #456"` in the expected notifications list
Both occurrences of `"Closed plan #456"` have been replaced with `"Closed PR #456"` using `replace_all=true`, ensuring consistency with the source code changes.
**Verification:** - Test function names remain unchanged (`test_tracks_notifications()`) - Docstrings are intact - Variable names are unchanged - The assertion in the `assert executor.notifications == [...]` list (line 40) now correctly expects `"Closed PR #456"`
The file is now consistent with the source code terminology shift from "plan" to "PR".
